name: Antihydrogen falls down in Earth's gravitational field
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict that antimatter responds to gravity the same way matter does, in agreement with the ALPHA-g 2023 measurement that cold antihydrogen atoms fall downward at 0.75 +/- 0.21 g in Earth's gravitational field. This decisively rules out 'antigravity' for antimatter (a_gbar / g = -1) and constrains composition-dependent equivalence-principle violations involving antimatter.

regime.description: Laboratory-scale free-fall of cold antihydrogen atoms (sub-millikelvin trapped in magnetic minimum, then released).

current_bound.units: in units of local g (downward = +1, upward = -1)
current_bound.reference: Anderson et al. (ALPHA Collaboration), 'Observation of the effect of gravity on the motion of antimatter', Nature 621, 716 (2023)

what_it_excludes: Theories predicting that antimatter has negative gravitational mass (antigravity, ruled out at high significance). Constrains gravitational-CPT violations and antimatter-specific fifth-force scenarios. Future improvements (ALPHA-g, AEgIS, GBAR) target precision approaching the matter-WEP precision (10^-15), at which point antihydrogen gravity becomes a strong test of CPT in the gravitational sector.

parameterization.name: Direct ratio a_gbar / g for antimatter in a gravitational field. In SME framework: gravitational-sector coefficients (a_eff)^mu and (s)^mu nu that distinguish matter from antimatter response.
parameterization.reference: Kostelecky and Tasson, PRD 83, 016013 (2011), arXiv:1006.4106 (gravitational SME).
parameterization.summary: CPT and the equivalence principle together predict a_gbar / g = +1 for any antimatter species. The SME parameterizes possible gravitational CPT violation through coefficients in the matter-graviton coupling Lagrangian.

procedural.predicted_observable: Free-fall acceleration of antihydrogen in a uniform gravitational field, measured in units of the local matter free-fall acceleration g.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: a_gbar / g = 0.75 +/- 0.21 (combined). Antigravity (a_gbar / g = -1) is excluded at high significance. Future ALPHA-g phase 2 + GBAR aim at percent-level precision; eventually atom-interferometry approaches may reach 10^-6 or better.
framework_inputs: If the framework distinguishes matter and antimatter gravitationally, compute a_gbar / g in a uniform external gravitational field.
Include any contributions from gravitational-sector SME coefficients or non-trivial CPT structure in the gravitational coupling.
procedural.notes: Some 'fourth-force' or 'gravivector' proposals predicted a_gbar < g without going to a_gbar = -g; future improvements will tighten these constraints as well.

primary_references.citation: Anderson et al. (ALPHA Collaboration), 'Observation of the effect of gravity on the motion of antimatter', Nature 621, 716 (2023)
Kostelecky and Tasson, 'Matter-Gravity Couplings and Lorentz Violation', PRD 83, 016013 (2011)
